message:
fix: increase graceful shutdown grace period from 100ms to 3s with polling (gt-wyayy)

The 100ms sleep after Ctrl-C was too short for any process to perform meaningful
graceful shutdown (saving files, committing, closing connections), making the
non-force path effectively identical to force-kill.

Changes:
- Add GracefulShutdownTimeout (3s) constant to constants package
- Add WaitForSessionExit() helper that polls for process exit instead of
  fixed sleep, returning early if the process exits before timeout
- Update all four shutdown paths: session/town.go, polecat/session_manager.go,
  dog/session_manager.go, cmd/down.go
- Add daemon.IsShutdownInProgress() exported function
- Add shutdown lock check to Boot triage to prevent Boot from restarting
  Deacon during the graceful shutdown window

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -26,6 +26,12 @@ const (
 
 	// PollInterval is the default polling interval for wait loops.
 	PollInterval = 100 * time.Millisecond
+
+	// GracefulShutdownTimeout is how long to wait after sending Ctrl-C before
+	// forcefully killing a session. 3 seconds gives processes time to perform
+	// meaningful graceful shutdown (saving files, committing, closing connections).
+	// The previous 100ms was effectively identical to a force-kill.
+	GracefulShutdownTimeout = 3 * time.Second
 )
 
 // Directory names within a Gas Town workspace.
